vulnerabilities.title: SQL Injection Vulnerability
vulnerabilities.code: const query = `SELECT * FROM users WHERE id = ${userId}`;
vulnerabilities.description: Direct string concatenation in SQL query allows SQL injection attacks. An attacker can manipulate the userId parameter to execute arbitrary SQL commands.
vulnerabilities.recommendation: Use parameterized queries or prepared statements

remediation.suggestedFix:
const query = 'SELECT * FROM users WHERE id = ?';
db.query(query, [userId], (err, results) => { ... });

vulnerabilities.title: Hardcoded Credentials
vulnerabilities.description: Database credentials are hardcoded in the source code. This exposes sensitive authentication information to anyone with access to the codebase.
vulnerabilities.recommendation: Store credentials in environment variables or secure vault

vulnerabilities.title: Command Injection
vulnerabilities.description: User-controlled input is directly passed to shell command execution. An attacker can inject malicious commands by manipulating the filename parameter.
vulnerabilities.recommendation: Validate and sanitize input, use safe alternatives to exec()

remediation.suggestedFix: Use child_process.spawn() with array arguments or validate filename against whitelist

vulnerabilities.title: Insecure Direct Object Reference (IDOR)
vulnerabilities.description: No authorization check before accessing documents. Users can access any document by guessing or enumerating document IDs.
vulnerabilities.recommendation: Implement proper authorization checks before file access

remediation.suggestedFix: Verify user ownership or permissions before serving files

vulnerabilities.title: Missing Authentication
vulnerabilities.description: Critical DELETE operation has no authentication or authorization checks. Any user can delete any user account.
vulnerabilities.recommendation: Implement authentication middleware and authorization checks

remediation.suggestedFix: Add authentication middleware and verify user permissions

vulnerabilities.title: Sensitive Data Exposure
vulnerabilities.description: Endpoint returns all user data including sensitive information like passwords without filtering.
vulnerabilities.recommendation: Filter sensitive fields before returning data

remediation.suggestedFix: Select only necessary fields or filter out sensitive data before response

vulnerabilities.title: Weak Cryptographic Hash (MD5)
vulnerabilities.description: MD5 is cryptographically broken and unsuitable for password hashing. Passwords can be easily cracked using rainbow tables or brute force.
vulnerabilities.recommendation: Use bcrypt, scrypt, or Argon2 for password hashing

vulnerabilities.title: Missing Input Validation
vulnerabilities.description: No validation of user input before processing. This can lead to data integrity issues and potential injection attacks.
vulnerabilities.recommendation: Implement input validation using a validation library

remediation.suggestedFix: Validate userData fields (email format, username length, etc.) before processing

vulnerabilities.title: Privilege Escalation Risk
vulnerabilities.description: No authorization check allows any user to escalate privileges by changing their role to admin.

remediation.suggestedFix: Verify that the requesting user has admin privileges before allowing role changes

vulnerabilities.title: SQL Injection in User Service
vulnerabilities.code: const query = `SELECT * FROM users WHERE email = '${email}'`;
vulnerabilities.description: String concatenation in SQL query allows SQL injection through the email parameter.

remediation.suggestedFix:
const query = 'SELECT * FROM users WHERE email = ?';
return db.query(query, [email]);

vulnerabilities.title: Cross-File Data Flow Vulnerability
vulnerabilities.description: Untrusted data flows from user input through UserService to database without proper sanitization across multiple files.
vulnerabilities.recommendation: Implement validation at service layer boundaries

remediation.suggestedFix: Add input validation and sanitization before database operations

vulnerabilities.title: Information Disclosure via Error Messages
vulnerabilities.description: Detailed error messages may expose sensitive information about database structure and queries.
vulnerabilities.recommendation: Implement proper error handling with generic error messages

remediation.suggestedFix: Log detailed errors server-side, return generic messages to clients

crossFileVulnerabilities.title: Weak Password Hash Propagation
crossFileVulnerabilities.description: Weakly hashed passwords from user_service.js are stored and used across the application
dataFlowChain: User input → UserService.createUser() → MD5 hash → Database → Authentication checks
crossFileVulnerabilities.impact: Compromised passwords can be used to access multiple parts of the application

crossFileVulnerabilities.title: Privilege Escalation Chain
crossFileVulnerabilities.description: Combination of missing authentication and role update function allows complete system compromise
crossFileVulnerabilities.attackScenario: Attacker can call updateUserRole without authentication to gain admin privileges, then access all protected resources

immediate: Fix all CRITICAL severity vulnerabilities (SQL Injection, Command Injection, Privilege Escalation)
Remove hardcoded credentials and use environment variables
shortTerm: Replace MD5 with bcrypt for password hashing
Add input validation across all endpoints
longTerm: Implement automated security testing in CI/CD
Add security headers and rate limiting
